summary: The branch is directionally solid and the self-review fixes are in good shape. However, there are several real issues: (1) a race condition in boot() where the watchdog toast fires before snapshot data can possibly be ready for slow connections; (2) the command-center.html redirect runs synchronously in a `<script>` tag with no `</script>` end yet relies on `return` in top-level script which is fragile; (3) the optimize-rpg-assets.mjs script queues source files for deletion even when the webp write hasn't been verified (no integrity check), and also has a delete-after-write atomicity gap where deletions happen in a final loop long after conversion—but critically, if `--delete-originals` is combined with `--dry-run` the deletion list is still populated and printed but nothing happens, which is correct, however the code adds to the `deletions` array even in DRY_RUN mode for the "upToDate" cached path, meaning the dry-run count is inflated by already-converted files (a minor logic issue); (4) `setView()` contains dead/redundant code that double-applies `.active` class toggles with a broken first pass; (5) the `_spokenTo` Set is initialised inside `renderSector()` rather than at state definition level, creating a subtle reset-on-re-render bug where re-renders can re-initialise the set; (6) mute keyboard handler is reachable even when a modal is open (the `return` in the modal block fires before the global `m/M` check, which is actually fine—but the Escape key global handler at the bottom is reachable when `state.view !== 'TITLE'` only if no modal/dialogue branch matched, which is correct). The most production-impactful issues are the DRY_RUN + delete atomicity gap, the setView double-render dead code (low risk but confusing), and the _spokenTo re-initialisation bug.

issues.fix:
Add `_spokenTo: new Set()` to the `state` object initialiser at line ~570:
```js
const state = {
  view: 'TITLE',
  focusedSector: 0,
  snapshot: null,
  snapshotErr: null,
  snapshotAt: 0,
  me: null,
  ccSessions: [],
  dialogue: null,
  modal: null,
  _spokenTo: new Set(),
  save: loadSave()
};
```
Then remove the `if (!state._spokenTo) state._spokenTo = new Set();` guard from `renderSector()`.

issues.description: Touch/mobile: the map hot-zones only handle `onclick` for entry. On touch devices, `onmouseenter` never fires, so hovering to change `focusedSector` before tapping doesn't work — the focused sector visually highlights only via `onclick` after the tap already committed. More importantly, there are no swipe gesture handlers anywhere; the only mobile navigation path is tap-to-enter which bypasses keyboard sector selection entirely. The `focusedSector` state therefore can be stale (pointing to whichever sector was last keyboard-navigated) when a touch user taps a different tile, though `onclick` does set `state.focusedSector = i` before calling `enterSector()` so the enter itself is correct.

`enterSector()` calls `persistSave()` twice in quick succession — once for `visited_sectors` and once for `last_visit_at`. Each call does a `localStorage.setItem` synchronously. This is harmless but wasteful.

`openWizardDialogue()` constructs dialogue body text by concatenating `s.last_action_summary` which comes from untrusted API data (`/api/cc-presence`). Although the text is assigned via `textContent` through the `el()` helper (which uses `textContent` for the `text` key), the `openDialogue` function sets `$('dialogueBody').textContent = visible` via the typewriter, which is safe. However `$('dialogueSpeaker').textContent = d.speaker` also uses textContent. All paths appear safe, but document this assumption so future maintainers don't switch to innerHTML.
